plan.visual_reading: The arc opens on Christ crying out from the cross, then reaches back to David writing the forsaken-man psalm. The proof movement is the ninth-hour darkness and the lifted cry 'My God, my God'. The conviction holds on the face that still calls God 'my God' even forsaken, and the substitution — the sinless Son bearing the forsaking our sin deserved. The landing turns toward a break of pale light behind the dark cross: forsaken so you never will be, the way home opened from the dark. The hero close is the crucified Christ against that breaking light.
plan.red_team_notes: Jaded Viewer risk: a generic 'sad Christ on a cross' could read as any passion image — so the cry is anchored to the SPECIFIC dereliction (head lifted, mouth open in the quoted cry) and the ninth-hour darkness, and the conviction face shows trust-amid-darkness (still 'my God'), not despair. The substitution scene (9) must NOT sever the Trinity — rendered as darkness/sin borne on Him, not the Father turning into a separate figure. Two scroll/David scenes (2, 12) kept distinct (writing vs the thousand-years diptych). Hero is the cross with breaking light (gospel-pivot), not the most anguished face.

scenes.title: The Cry
scenes.rationale: the specific quoted dereliction, not a generic passion image
scenes.visible_elements: the crucified Christ's lifted face, mouth open in a cry, eyes toward a dark sky
scenes.subject_block: a close view of the crucified Christ's face lifted toward a darkened sky, the mouth open in a single desolate cry, the brow furrowed beneath a crown of thorns, tear-tracked cheeks in faint cold light against deep black shadow, a fold of cream robe and the dark grain of the cross beam at the shoulder
macro_elements: a tear track on the cheek

scenes.title: David's Forsaken Psalm
scenes.purpose: The psalm of the forsaken man, written early
scenes.rationale: David writing in the voice of a forsaken man (Ps 22:1)
scenes.visible_elements: aged David alone at a scroll in shadow, an oil lamp, faint background vignettes of forsakenness
scenes.subject_block: an aged King David seated alone at a low table in deep shadow, a feather pen at rest above a half-unrolled parchment of Hebrew, a single small lamp throwing a thin light across his lined, sorrowful face, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting a forsaken man with bowed head, an empty dark horizon, a far wooden cross, and the wet first line of Hebrew
vignettes: a forsaken man with bowed head
the wet first line of Hebrew

scenes.title: The First Line
scenes.rationale: a 2s insert on the written Ps 22:1
scenes.visible_elements: extreme close on a Hebrew scroll line, a fingertip beneath it
scenes.subject_block: an extreme close view of an open parchment, a single weathered fingertip at rest beneath the first illuminated line of ancient Hebrew script, the ink old and cracked, the parchment fibrous and dimly lit, deep shadow at the edges

scenes.title: The Ninth Hour
scenes.rationale: the ninth-hour darkness over the cross (Matt 27:45)
scenes.visible_elements: the crucified Christ on the cross under a darkened noon sky
scenes.subject_block: the crucified Christ on a rough wooden cross seen from a low angle against a black, unnatural noon sky, robed at the waist, head lifted, a thin cold light on the pale robe while the land below sinks into darkness, a darkened sun low in the murk
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of cosmic dread at midday darkness
macro_elements: the lifted head against the black sky
the thin cold light on the robe

scenes.title: My God, My God
scenes.rationale: the face in the act of the quoted cry
scenes.visible_elements: Christ's face lifted, mouth open, eyes toward heaven
scenes.subject_block: a close view of the crucified Christ's face turned upward, the mouth open around the cry, the eyes lifted toward a dark heaven with an anguished, searching appeal, a crown of thorns on the brow, faint light on the upturned face against deep shadow
macro_elements: the cords of the strained neck

scenes.title: Darkness Over the Land
scenes.purpose: the darkened land at the cross
scenes.rationale: Matt 27:45 darkness over all the land
scenes.visible_elements: the cross on the hill under a black sky, a darkened land, distant figures
scenes.subject_block: a wide view of three crosses on a dark hill beneath a black noon sky, the central crucified Christ small and pale at the heart of it, the land below swallowed in unnatural dusk, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting the darkened sun, fleeing figures on the road, a watching centurion, and the shadowed city walls
macro_elements: the pale central figure on the cross

scenes.title: The Same Words
scenes.rationale: the Hebrew line of Ps 22:1 against the dark of the cross
scenes.visible_elements: the Hebrew first line of the psalm, the dark cross beam beyond
scenes.subject_block: a close view of the first line of Hebrew on an old parchment held in faint light, the dark grain of a cross beam looming in the shadow beyond, the worn ink sharp against the pale fibre, deep darkness at the edges
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of solemn recognition across the years

scenes.title: Still 'My God'
scenes.rationale: even forsaken He still calls God 'my God' — not despair
scenes.visible_elements: Christ's anguished face with eyes still lifted in trust
scenes.subject_block: a close view of the crucified Christ's face, the eyes lifted and holding toward the dark heaven with a steady, trusting appeal even in anguish, the brow strained beneath the crown of thorns, a faint warm light catching the upturned face against the surrounding black
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of faith holding in the darkness
macro_elements: the faint warm light on the cheek

scenes.title: Bearing the Forsaking
scenes.purpose: the substitution — bearing the forsaking our sin deserved
scenes.rationale: penal substitution as darkness/sin borne, not Trinity severed
scenes.visible_elements: Christ on the cross bearing the dark, the light turned away
scenes.subject_block: the crucified Christ at the heart of a deep darkness, head bowed, the pale robe the only light in a swallowing black, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting a weight of dark shadow gathered upon his shoulders, a light turned away beyond the hill, a spotless white lamb in the gloom, and broken chains at the foot of the cross
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of judgment borne in the place of another
macro_elements: a spotless lamb in the gloom
vignettes: a weight of dark shadow on his shoulders
a light turned away beyond the hill
a spotless white lamb in the gloom
broken chains at the foot of the cross

scenes.title: So You Never Will Be
scenes.rationale: the forsaken face as a faint break of light begins
scenes.visible_elements: Christ's face turned up, a faint break of pale light
scenes.emotional_tone: the turn from dark to grace
scenes.subject_block: a close view of the crucified Christ's face turned gently upward, the crown of thorns above, the anguish softening as a faint break of pale light begins to touch the brow against the receding dark, deep shadow still at the edges
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of the first turn from darkness to grace
macro_elements: the first break of pale light on the brow

scenes.title: The Way Opened from the Dark
scenes.rationale: the cross with a break of light — the way home opened
scenes.visible_elements: the crucified Christ on the cross, a break of pale light behind the dark hill
scenes.subject_block: the crucified Christ on a rough wooden cross from a respectful low angle, robed at the waist, head gently inclined, a widening break of pale gold light breaking behind the dark hill and the black sky, the land below still in shadow, the way out of the dark opening behind him
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a costly, open welcome out of the dark
macro_elements: the widening break of pale gold light

scenes.title: A Thousand Years Apart
scenes.rationale: David's forsaken psalm and the ninth-hour cross across a thousand years
scenes.visible_elements: David at his scroll at left, the darkened cross at right
scenes.subject_block: a wide two-sided composition split by a corridor of deep shadow; at the left, aged David seated alone at a thin lamp over a wet-inked scroll; at the right, across the divide, the crucified Christ on a cross beneath a black noon sky, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting the first Hebrew line, a bowed forsaken head, the darkened sun, and the far hill of the cross
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of solemn wonder across a thousand years
vignettes: the far hill of the cross

scenes.title: However Far You've Run
scenes.purpose: the way home for the listener
scenes.rationale: a lone figure on a dark road turning toward the far light
scenes.visible_elements: a lone distant figure on a dark road, a far break of light by a cross
scenes.subject_block: a wide view of a lone, small figure standing on a dark road across an empty shadowed land, turned toward a distant break of pale light where a far cross stands on the horizon, the foreground in deep shadow, the far light the only warmth
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of the long way home opening
macro_elements: the distant cross on the horizon

scenes.title: He Opened It from the Dark
scenes.purpose: the way opened, in one detail
scenes.rationale: the nailed hand against a break of light
scenes.visible_elements: a nailed open hand against a break of pale light
scenes.subject_block: an extreme close view of a single nailed open hand against the rough grain of the cross beam, the iron nail through the palm, the fingers relaxed and open, a break of pale gold light widening in the dark beyond the hand, a thin dark line of blood, warm light on the knuckles
scenes.mood_block: atmosphere of a way costly opened from the dark
macro_elements: the iron nail in the palm
the break of pale light beyond

plan.rationale: Anchored the hook to the SPECIFIC quoted cry (lifted head, open mouth) and the ninth-hour darkness rather than a generic passion; guarded the substitution scene (9) against Trinity-severing by rendering it as darkness/sin borne, not the Father as a separate figure; the conviction face (8) shows trust-amid-darkness ('still my God'), not despair; hero is the cross with breaking light (11), the gospel-pivot, not the most anguished face.

panel.note: Scene 1 'The Cry' opens on the specific quoted dereliction; coherent arc cry -> psalm -> ninth-hour darkness -> substitution -> way home.
Scene 8 'Still My God' guards against the despair reading; scene 9 'Bearing the Forsaking' renders substitution as darkness borne, not a severed Trinity — orthodox.
State-only, Kling-cuttable subject_blocks; no banned tokens; macro_elements present.
Many passion-Christ scenes — render with the passion ref for face consistency.
Hero #11 is the cross with breaking light (gospel-pivot), not the most anguished face.
Several anguished-face scenes risk sameness — framings (low-angle, wide, close) and the light-turn differentiate them.

paper_cohesion.notes: The set forms a coherent visual spine on the dereliction thread: it opens on the quoted cry (1), establishes David's forsaken psalm (2,3), lands the ninth-hour darkness and the lifted 'My God, my God' (4,5,6,7), holds the trust-amid-darkness face and the borne substitution (8,9), then turns to a break of light and the way home opened from the dark (10,11,13,14). No theology drift: scene 8 explicitly denies the despair reading (eyes still lifted in trust), and scene 9 renders substitution as darkness/sin borne on Christ rather than the Father severed from the Son. Every narration beat is carried by at least one strong scene; hook->climax->close (the cry -> the ninth-hour darkness -> the cross with breaking light) is a clean spine. Hero #11 is the gospel-pivot cross, so the cut lands on Christ and the opened way home.
